name: L'Antica Pizzeria da Michele
hours: Mon-Thu: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Fri-Sat: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sun: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM
teaser: Classic Neapolitan pies that are fairly close to the Naples original, and the sceney space might be the nicest room to eat pizza in Los Angeles.
address: 1534 N McCadden Pl, Los Angeles, CA 90028, USA
review: The pizza at this franchise offshoot of the famous Naples pizzeria of the same name is roughly the same. They're using most of the same Italian ingredients, but some things (like cheese) get exponentially better when sourced fresh near the origin. And though Julia Roberts fell in love with the no-frills Italian version, I actually prefer the sceney space in Hollywood, which is probably the nicest place to eat a pie in the city. The courtyard feels like an oasis in the middle of a tourist trap; the main room is dark but warm and homey. I only wish the classic Neapolitan pies got a slight upgrade the way the decor did. They're good but slightly dated.
